Container.Name: restorerunner
Container.Overview:
Automated backup restore rehearsal — proves your backup is still restorable, not just that the job ran.
PRE-RELEASE BETA. Actively testing; report issues on GitHub.
How it works: - Enrol a Restic / Borg / Kopia repo; RestoreRunner walks your live source and records a baseline snapshot (file paths, sizes, mtimes, SHA-256 hashes). - On a schedule (weekly default), it samples files from the baseline, pulls them from the backup, hashes them, and compares bit-for-bit. - Alerts via Apprise (ntfy, Gotify, Discord, Telegram, 110+ channels).
Unlike 'restic check' or 'kopia verify', this answers the question: 'if I had to restore right now, would the files actually come back bit-for-bit identical to the source?'.
Setup (one-off after first start): 1. Edit /etc/restorerunner/config.yml (add repos) 2. Generate auth hash: read -rs PW; echo -n "$PW" | docker exec -i restorerunner /usr/local/bin/restorerunner hashpw 3. Enrol each repo: docker exec -i restorerunner /usr/local/bin/restorerunner enroll --config /etc/restorerunner/config.yml <name>
Container.DonateText: If you like this, please consider a donation!
Container.Description: Automated backup restore rehearsal for Restic, Borg, and Kopia. Periodically pulls a random sample of files from your backup, hashes them, and proves they match the source. PRE-RELEASE BETA.

Config.Name: Config
Config.Description: config.yml and any password files (must be chmod 600)

Config.Name: Data
Config.Description: SQLite database with enrollment baselines and rehearsal history

Config.Name: Backup source (read-only)
Config.Description: One of the source trees your backups cover. Add more mounts as needed (same for each additional tree).

Config.Name: Backup repository (read-only)
Config.Description: Your Restic/Borg/Kopia repository. Duplicate this row for each repo.

Config.Name: Timezone
Config.Description: Container timezone (used for cron scheduling of rehearsals)
